evidence_quotes.quote: I would request inclusion of digital media like video games to be part of your initiative.

The digital products that the consumer pays for can be stripped away from then at any moment, without so much as a warning or even a single regard to the personal & monetary investment of said consumer.
Currently companies can just force the paying customers to hand over their digital license and destroy it completely without any consequence.

paragraphs.text: Esteemed members, thank you for this initiative and the protection you bring to the consumer. Especially in these uncertain times where major corporations are overtaking whole sectors and creating extremely parasitical environments for their own customers. I truly believe that standing together is of upmost importance before this issue rises to unapproachable scale. As such I would request inclusion of digital media like video games to be part of your initiative. The digital products that the consumer pays for can be stripped away from then at any moment, without so much as a warning or even a single regard to the personal & monetary investment of said consumer. I do not wish to see video games, or any other digital media for that fact, be lost into nothingness without any end of life plan that keeps the consumer in mind. Such media in my opinion corresponds to an art form where many hard working developers have put in their heart and soul to make something they are proud of, and it would be a waste for it to be taken away permanently without every witnessing the light of day again. Currently companies can just force the paying customers to hand over their digital license and destroy it completely without any consequence. This is text-book planned obsolescence that is not good for anyone but the corporations, and strips all power away from the consumer. I hope you will take digital media into account as well as you would with physical products and create a future where consumers own what they pay for without the fear of losing it in a moment's notice. There has been a European citizen's initiative recently that covers these problems more in depth called "Stop Killing Games", for if a clearer scope of the matter would aid your understanding of my position. Thank you very much and keep up the good fight. The consumers support your cause and have got your back!
